key_insight: STRAND reframes persistence diagrams as survival data, enabling both hypothesis testing and stable vector representations from the same interpretable persistence survival function.
engineering_relevance: This gives practitioners a practical way to compare topological summaries statistically while also turning them into features for machine learning pipelines.
method: Treat each persistence value p = d - b as a fully observed survival time.
Build the persistence survival function S(t) = P(p > t) as the common representation.
Derive a non-parametric two-sample test, effect sizes, and a 1-Wasserstein-stable vectorization from S(t).
evidence_signal: The abstract reports calibrated Type I error, high power on synthetic manifolds, competitive results on 14 graph and 3 point-cloud benchmarks, and an application to fMRI brain connectivity.
watch_item: Performance depends on how persistence diagrams are sampled and discretized into the survival representation, so calibration and stability should be checked for the target dataset.
paper_title: From Persistence to Survival: Hypothesis Testing, Effect Sizes and Vectorisation for Topological Features
